text:
<think>I should clarify that it is inaccurate to say all Mexicans are members of a single church, as Mexico is religiously diverse.</think>
Not all Mexicans belong to the same church, as Mexico is home to people of various religious beliefs. 
<think>Now, I should mention the largest religious group in Mexico for context, while emphasizing it is not universal.</think>
However, the majority of Mexicans identify as Roman Catholic, which has historically been the predominant religion in the country.
<think>I should further note the presence of other religious affiliations to provide a complete and accurate answer.</think>
There are also significant populations of Protestants, members of other Christian denominations, and people who follow other religions or have no religious affiliation.
<think>I should conclude by reiterating that religious identity in Mexico is diverse and cannot be attributed to a single church for all citizens.</think>
Therefore, it is incorrect to generalize that all Mexicans are members of any one church.
